core.text:
Actually I'll keep this reply short and just dm you. Basically fmp your friend/enemy distinction is true and important because it leads you to courage and bravery in the name of building something positive. Yes, the darkness and depravity you see in the left is really there. I think you can feel good about making yourself ready to meet the challenge. 

But it's also true that the world is not made of a spectrum from friends to enemies. The world is not divided in this way. The entire world is you and has something to teach you, including leftists. So the darkness and depravity you are certain you see in leftists is in some way an illusion that reveals something about you you haven't seen before. You can harden yourself now, but you won't truly win the war unless you fully surrender the victory. That's because the war will transform you and your enemy in ways you don't know yet.

Being both Caesar and Christ is not really about showing mercy. It's not about mercy because that means you don't see the value in the evil of leftists. What it's really about is fighting the battle while surrendering wanting to win from within because you don't know that you are good and they are evil, or who you are. That basically means that I think the war between you and leftists is good and meaningful and the immense pain you and your friends and Charlie Kirk have felt and will feel is good and meaningful and I hope you have the courage to feel it fully. This war and its pain has something to teach both you and the leftists. That doesn't mean there isn't good or evil. I hope you win. This is beyond good and evil.

It's good and important to fight to build order. This is what I wanted to do the whole time. I had enemies who were in the way of this order and I wished they would be gone. When I was fully ready to die, metaphorically and literally, when I got to the point where I didn't know anything including my life, I found out that it's all been okay the whole time. The battle is important but nothing is more important than peace and love. Love is more powerful than all enemies, it is the source of all order, beyond all order, so you'd best love your enemy. Don't have mercy on them, love them as you destroy them. 

The easy way is to face these battles with courage and surrender from within, making yourself your enemy. This is why I say the entire world is you, and there are no enemies. How do leftists make you feel? Can you love that feeling? If you can, you still have to fight the leftists do your duty, but isn't it a lot easier?

I want you to have an easy way because I think I understand some of who you are, and I relate to it deeply. I have lived a very similar life to you. My battles are over now though. I suspect that you will go the hard way by thinking that fighting this battle without is ultimately important. I respect your pagan beliefs and think its an important part of whatever comes next, but I suspect that they overemphasize courage in battle, and don't understand that surrender is ultimately superior. I think you probably started this journey because you were looking for love, and I'll just say in order to find it you may have to love your enemy. It would be funny if your future wife or something ends up being a leftist pagan and love has that sense of humor.

I'm not a Christian btw. I used to be a Hindu, then an ahteist American liberal, and now basically just tpot, which I guess is just hinduism. But I'm not really any of these things, I just destroyed myself to find love, and when I was ready to die, I did.

that you are probably seeing a more skewed representation of bloodlusty leftists than there are in reality because of social media. A lot of this is still structural and you can be friends with leftists.

But that's neither interesting nor useful. I'd like to wish you more than just good luck, and share some relevant parts of my journey.

I've almost certainly had a very similar life to you, spiritually if not literally...

I understand. Thanks for engaging so openly.
